page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. Operating at 2.8 GHz, this instance features 64 physical cores with a dedicated single thread per core allocation, providing 64 vCPUs. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-focused ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory throughput. The instance achieves top-tier results in memory bandwidth tests for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks, alongside a top-tier Passmark memory score. Multi-threaded operations perform exceptionally well, as demonstrated by top-tier scores in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation with Clang, and Bzip3 decompression. Database and in-memory operations are highly efficient, with top-tier performance in Redis SET operations and Passmark database operations. However, single-core CPU benchmarks, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench text processing, fall into the average category.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARMv9 architecture and dedicated core allocation deliver high resource density and exceptional memory bandwidth per resource unit, making it highly competitive for multi-threaded workloads. The absence of local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, focusing resources entirely on network-bound and compute-bound tasks. This profile is highly suited for high-throughput network applications, static web serving, software compilation, multi-threaded data processing, and medium-scale LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and 64-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. Operating at 2.8 GHz with dedicated single-thread cores, it features 128.0 GB of memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding throughput in Redis SET operations, software compilation, and static web serving. Conversely, single-core performance remains average. Qualitatively, the ARM64 architecture offers high resource density and memory efficiency for parallel workloads. The c8gn.16xlarge is highly suited for network-intensive applications, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is a compute-optimized ARM64 server with 64 Graviton physical cores, 128 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.16xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 64 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance delivering high-bandwidth networking, exceptional memory throughput, and dedicated multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz.
Equipped with 128.0 GB of memory and a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps.
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing performance.
Optimized for static web serving, software compilation, and Redis database operations.
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage.
